queued_publication_packets.publication_if_solved: A decisive existence or nonexistence result for the cyclic (990,345,120) case would read like the title theorem of a short residual-case note tied to Gordon's small open cyclic...
queued_publication_packets.canonical_source: Daniel M. Gordon, "The La Jolla Difference Set Repository" (ArasuFest talk slides, August 3, 2019), especially the Ryser-conjecture slide listing (990,345,120,225) among the small open cyclic cases and the Lander-conjecture slide listing the exact 990 row [2,G9,5,11].

queued_publication_packets.publication_if_solved: A decisive result on the cyclic (936,375,150) row would look close to a short residual-case note, but the present packet stops just short of the 70-90% paper threshold.
queued_publication_packets.canonical_source: Daniel M. Gordon, "The La Jolla Difference Set Repository" (ArasuFest talk slides, August 3, 2019), especially the Ryser-conjecture slide listing (936,375,150,225) among the small open cyclic cases and the Lander-conjecture slide listing exact noncyclic 936 rows.

queued_publication_packets.publication_if_solved: A direct solve of the exact [2,4,9,13] row would be close to a note, but it still risks reading as only one cell in a multi-row 936 classification packet.
queued_publication_packets.canonical_source: Daniel M. Gordon, "The La Jolla Difference Set Repository" (ArasuFest talk slides, August 3, 2019), especially the Lander-conjecture slide listing the exact open row 936 375 150 225 [2,4,9,13] with p = 3.

queued_publication_packets.publication_if_solved: A direct solve of the exact [8,9,13] row would be close to a short note, but it still carries high isolated-case risk inside the wider 936 packet.
queued_publication_packets.canonical_source: Daniel M. Gordon, "The La Jolla Difference Set Repository" (ArasuFest talk slides, August 3, 2019), especially the Lander-conjecture slide listing the exact open row 936 375 150 225 [8,9,13] with p = 3.

queued_publication_packets.publication_if_solved: An exact result for the [2,224] row could support a short note, but the present packet is still too thin on later-status support and falls below the target paper fraction.
queued_publication_packets.canonical_source: Daniel M. Gordon and Bernhard Schmidt, "A Survey of the Multiplier Conjecture" (Designs, Codes and Cryptography 78, 2016), especially Table 2 listing the exact open row (448,150,50) in group [2,224].
